cell_authoring_guidance: Each cell renders inside a sandboxed iframe whose body inherits the canvas theme's --background color. ANY pixels your HTML doesn't visually cover will show that background through.

When using a primitive as the outermost element of a grid cell, wrap it in `<div class="h-full ...">` (or apply h-full directly to the primitive) so it fills the cell's full vertical space. Otherwise the cell's body background shows below your content as empty space.

Match the visual language of the chosen theme. If the canvas is using a 'light' theme but your design wants dark surfaces, switch the theme to 'default'/'amber'/'ocean' (all dark) via canvas_apply_theme rather than fighting it with per-cell overrides — half-overridden themes leak in empty regions.

Don't rely on Tailwind arbitrary-value classes like `bg-[#FF1493]` — Flynt ships a curated Tailwind subset that doesn't include the JIT compiler. Stick to theme-token classes (`bg-primary`, `text-foreground`, `border-border`) plus the included spacing/layout/typography utilities.

Cell sizing: prefer h: 1 for single-row content (badges, buttons, headlines), h: 2 for headers with subhead, h: 3+ for content-heavy cards. A tall cell with short content will show empty body background — either reduce h or expand content to fill.

primitives.name: Button
primitives.usage_notes:
Inline-flex element. Center inside a cell with a flex parent: `
`.
primitives.html: <button class="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-sm font-medium px-4 py-2 bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:opacity-90 transition">Click me</button>

primitives.name: Button (secondary)
primitives.description: Secondary action button with muted styling.
primitives.usage_notes: Same layout posture as primary button — needs a flex parent for cell centering.
primitives.html: <button class="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-sm font-medium px-4 py-2 bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground border border-border hover:opacity-90 transition">Cancel</button>

primitives.name: Button (ghost)
primitives.usage_notes: Same layout posture as primary button.
primitives.html: <button class="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-sm font-medium px-4 py-2 text-foreground hover:bg-muted transition">More</button>

primitives.name: Input
primitives.usage_notes:
Block element with w-full input. Wrap in `
…
` if used as a cell's outermost element so it fills the cell.

primitives.name: Textarea
primitives.usage_notes: Block element. Pair with a parent that has explicit padding and h-full for cell use.

primitives.name: Card
primitives.description: Card with header, body, and footer slots.
primitives.usage_notes: When used as a cell's outermost element, ADD `h-full` to the card's root div so it fills the cell's vertical space. Otherwise the cell's body background shows below the card as empty space — this is the most common cause of unwanted whitespace in canvas designs.
primitives.html:
Card title

A short description.

Card content goes here.

primitives.name: Badge
primitives.usage_notes: Inline element — flow with text or use multiple in a flex container with `gap-2`.
primitives.html: Beta

primitives.name: Alert
primitives.description: Inline notification with title and description.
primitives.usage_notes: Block element — pairs naturally with `h-full` to fill a cell. The alert's own background covers the cell body.
primitives.html:

You can add components and dependencies to your app using the cli.

primitives.name: Separator
primitives.usage_notes: 1px high. Place between sibling content within a cell, not as the cell's outermost element.

primitives.name: Label
primitives.usage_notes: Inline. Pair with associated input via flex-col + gap.

primitives.name: Switch
primitives.description: On/off toggle (visual only — wire onclick if interactive).
primitives.usage_notes: Static visual — needs onclick + state in cell.js if you want it interactive.
